**FORMAL SPECIFICATION**

**Symbols · Characters · Talonics**

**ERES Cryptographic Stack Primitive**

*GCF–EDF–USC Integration with (CBGMODD×FAVORS)+BERA=RATE Interlock*

*Operational Tie-Ins: UBIMIA · NBERS · PlayNAC   |   Heritage: Trifurcated Math*

| Document ID | ERES-CRYPTO-SPEC-2026-001 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Version** | 1.3 (SCT framing; VEILED rename; Trifurcated Math heritage; supersedes v1.2) |
| **Date** | April 21, 2026 |
| **Author** | Joseph Allen Sprute (ERES Maestro / JAS) |
| **Organization** | ERES Institute for New Age Cybernetics, Bella Vista, AR |
| **ORCID** | 0000-0001-9946-3221 |
| **ResearchGate** | Target slot RG \#425 (following RG \#424) |
| **Phase** | Consolidation Phase → upstream of VERTECA |
| **Governs** | SECUIR cryptographic output layer |
| **Status** | **Locked and tested. Ten.** |
| **License** | CCAL v2.1 (CARE Commons Attribution License) |
| **Validation** | MIEVM (Claude, Grok, DeepSeek, ChatGPT) |

**Abstract**

The ERES Cryptographic Stack introduces a primitive class in which authentication and authorization are achieved through resonance-keyed semantic superposition rather than computational hardness. Three polysemantic primitives — GCF (Greatest Common Factor / GraceChain Formula / Good Christ Family), EDF (Earned Dignity Framework / Earth Defense Force / Entity Data Framework), and USC (Utterance–Sound–Cognition, 3×3 \= 9 semantic dimensions) — each hold multiple simultaneous readings that collapse to a single operational reading only under a valid resonance signature. The stack is bound by a single canonical interlock, (CBGMODD × FAVORS) \+ BERA \= RATE, shown here to be isomorphic to ERES Triune Math canonical form \#2 (M×E+C=R). Output is a seven-dimensional uncollapsed RATE vector; scalar collapse is prohibited. A semantically-unresolvable RATE dimension is named VEILED (notation ⊥) per ERES canon, consistent with CyberRAVE (“Cybernetic Ratings Abolishing Veiled Exchanges”) and the HPE DESCENT taxonomy. A four-mode failure taxonomy distinguishes governance, biometric, resonance, and semantic-collision failures. The stack is operationally bound to three downstream ERES instruments: GCF gates UBIMIA benefit flow; BERA feeds NBERS (non-punitive behavioral evaluation and remediation); and EarnedPath \+ GERP establishes the context through which PlayNAC navigates its 126-state governance space (18 Layers × 7 Levels). The seven-level cardinality of PlayNAC matches the seven-dimension RATE output by design. Four numbered security claims are stated. This specification v1.3 reframes the stack within the Symbols-Characters-Talonics (SCT) organizing triad, formalizes the Trifurcated Math lineage (establishing \`example × use \+ multiplier \= answers\` as a third pedagogical isomorph of Triune Math \#2), and renames the formerly ⊥-notated state to VEILED.

**Keywords**

Symbols-Characters-Talonics (SCT); Talonics; Trifurcated Math; VEILED; resonance-keyed cryptography; polysemantic primitives; semantic superposition; biometric attestation; governance substrate; conjunctive gate; Triune Math; ERES; GAIA; Meritcoin; Proof-of-Resonance; UBIMIA; NBERS; PlayNAC; EarnedPath; GERP; multidimensional authorization; VERTECA; SECUIR

# **1\. Scope and Purpose**

This specification defines the ERES Cryptographic Stack — a novel cryptographic primitive class in which authentication and authorization are achieved not through computational hardness but through resonance-keyed semantic superposition. Three polysemantic primitives (GCF, EDF, USC) each carry multiple simultaneous readings; a correct resonance signature collapses the superposition to the intended reading. Without the correct signature, the readings remain superposed and the primitive yields no operationally resolved meaning.

The stack is bound together by a single canonical interlock:

**(CBGMODD × FAVORS) \+ BERA \= RATE**

This form is structurally isomorphic to ERES Triune Math \#2 (M×E+C=R). The cryptographic layer is therefore not a parallel construction but an instance of the Triune Math operating in the authentication domain.

## **1.1 Classification within the ERES Decision-Stack**

* Consolidation Phase deliverable (not a Research Phase preliminary).

* Upstream dependency of VERTECA (voice portal semantic interpretation layer).

* Governs the cryptographic output layer of SECUIR.

* Operationally tied to UBIMIA (via GCF), NBERS (via BERA), and PlayNAC (via EP+GERP). See §12.

## **1.2 What This Specification Is Not**

* Not a hardness-based cryptosystem.

* Not a replacement for transport-layer cryptography.

* Not a scoring rubric. RATE is an uncollapsed multidimensional output; it cannot be reduced to a single scalar.

# **2\. The Symbols-Characters-Talonics Frame**

This specification is positioned within the ERES Symbols-Characters-Talonics (SCT) frame. SCT is the organizing triad for ERES notational artifacts:

* **Symbols —** abstract token labels that identify primitives, operators, and states. In the crypto stack: GCF, EDF, USC, CBGMODD, FAVORS, BERA, RATE, VEILED.

* **Characters —** individual readings or values within a symbol. In the crypto stack: G₁/G₂/G₃ for GCF; E₁/E₂/E₃ for EDF; the nine cells of USC; the attestation values of CBGMODD pillars; the BERA indices; the R₁–R₇ dimensions; the modes F₁–F₄.

* **Talonics —** the formal notational system that expresses both Symbols and Characters. Grounded in the ERES Talonics Symbol Matrix (452 elements, 12 parts; derived as 72 × 2π ≈ 452\) and the Talonics Proof-of-Work (RG \#397) establishing TETRA:GAIA\_Primitive and the DALEMAJAS tensor. Talonics is what lets Symbols and Characters be written, manipulated, and transmitted under a canonical notation.

The crypto stack is therefore not a standalone construction; it is an instance of SCT applied to authentication and authorization. Specifications in adjacent domains — governance, certification, remediation, data integrity — will share this frame. The SCT triad is itself a trifurcation in the Trifurcated Math sense (§6.6), and the frame is therefore recursive: the notation that expresses the crypto stack is itself governed by the same structural law that governs the stack’s output.

## **2.1 Why SCT, and Why Here**

Reviewers encountering ERES for the first time often read the crypto stack as a speculative construction or as a metaphor for existing cryptography. The SCT frame resolves this by naming the category to which the stack belongs. The stack is a cryptographic SCT artifact: it uses specific Symbols (the primitive labels), enumerates specific Characters (the readings), and is transcribable in Talonics. Other ERES artifacts belong to the same category with different content. Polysemantic primitives are not unique to this specification; they are the canonical form of ERES Symbols when the Symbol admits multiple simultaneous readings.

## **2.2 Interaction with the Rest of This Document**

All subsequent sections should be read as SCT content. When §6 enumerates the polysemantic primitives, it is enumerating Symbols and their Characters. When §9 Matrix Modulation discusses Μ(ctx), that operator is itself a Symbol with attested Characters. When §6.6 traces the Trifurcated Math lineage, it is showing that the pedagogical form of ERES teaching is a simpler instance of the same SCT pattern formalized here.

# **3\. Normative References**

Compliant implementations shall resolve all references to the specified version or later.

* ERES-RECKON-WP-2026-002 — Seven Resonance Anchors; conjunctive gate.

* ERES-RECKON-MATH-WP-2026-002a — ERES Triune Math canonical forms.

* ERES-BODY-SPEC-2026-001 — GAIA BODY substrate specification.

* ERES-BRAINS-SPEC-2026-001 — BRAINS Corpus Ingestion Pipeline.

* ERES TERMS \#47 (ET\_AL) — Locked Terms Registry; Three Governing Principles.

* ERES-COVENANT-2026-001 — ERES Equity Covenant (1000-year governance).

* ERES NPR v3.0 (sealed) and NPR v4.0 / NBERS pilot — non-punitive remediation framework.

* ERES SCALULAR Engine Document — four-pillar certification architecture and UBIMIA disbursement basis.

* ERES Talonics Symbol Matrix (452 elements, 12 parts).

* Talonics Proof-of-Work (RG \#397) — TETRA:GAIA\_Primitive.

# **4\. Related Work and Positioning**

ERES Crypto departs from several established families of cryptographic and identity primitives. This section positions the stack against each and states the basis of novelty.

## **4.1 Computational-Hardness Cryptosystems**

RSA, ECDSA, and lattice-based systems rest on presumed intractability of factorization, discrete logarithm, or shortest-vector problems. ERES Crypto does not rely on hardness. Its security property is not “an adversary cannot compute” but “an adversary cannot produce a joint resonance context.” This is a semantic condition rather than a computational one, and it is not threatened by advances in classical or quantum computation.

## **4.2 Zero-Knowledge Proofs and SNARKs**

ZK systems let a prover demonstrate knowledge without revealing it. ERES Crypto instead lets a prover demonstrate resonance without declaring intent. ZK protects information; ERES Crypto protects semantic intent. The two are composable but solve different problems.

## **4.3 Biometric Multifactor Authentication**

Standard MFA composes independent factors. FAVORS is a six-channel biometric operator within the inherence factor, but it is subordinated to governance (CBGMODD) and resonance (BERA) layers above it. Pure biometric MFA produces binary pass/fail; ERES Crypto produces a seven-dimensional RATE vector encoding quality of authentication, not mere success.

## **4.4 Self-Sovereign Identity and Decentralized Identifiers**

SSI and DID frameworks give subjects control over identity assertions. ERES Crypto is compatible with SSI at the CBGMODD layer but adds a resonance attestation that SSI systems do not natively provide. This addresses intent laundering and coercion-at-runtime scenarios pure SSI cannot.

## **4.5 Consensus Mechanisms**

Proof-of-work and proof-of-stake are consensus protocols, not identity/authorization primitives. ERES Meritcoin uses Proof-of-Resonance, which accrues value from validated ethical and ecological harmony rather than from computational or financial expenditure.

## **4.6 Basis of Novelty**

The novel contribution is a new primitive class — resonance-keyed semantic superposition — that operates at the intersection of governance, biometric, and resonance layers. The stack is the first cryptographic primitive to bind intent to authentication without requiring an explicit declaration-of-intent field, and it is the first cryptographic artifact expressed as a Symbols-Characters-Talonics construction (§2).

# **5\. Definitions and Locked Terms**

Terms in this section are locked per ERES TERMS \#47. Divergence constitutes a specification violation.

| Term | Locked Definition |
| :---- | :---- |
| **GCF** | Polysemantic primitive: (1) Greatest Common Factor — mathematical reduction; (2) GraceChain Formula — governance formula; (3) Good Christ Family — familial-spiritual anchor. Gates UBIMIA disbursement. |
| **EDF** | Polysemantic primitive: (1) Earned Dignity Framework (ONE-GOOD); (2) Earth Defense Force (SECURITY-CLEARANCE); (3) Entity Data Framework (DATA-INTEGRITY — tied to GAIA ENTITY). Validates resonance across three operational domains. All three leading tokens are distinct (Earned / Earth / Entity), per the polysemantic uniqueness rule (§5.5). |
| **USC** | Utterance–Sound–Cognition. 3×3 \= 9 semantic dimensions. U: Unite, Universe, Us. S: Safe, Sound, Solid. C: Constant, Care, Christ. |
| **CBGMODD** | Scalable Certification Architecture governance operator. Four pillars: HEALTH/SSHP, LAW/SSLA, PROTECTION/SSPS, SKILLS\_TRADE/SSST. Tier 1 \= SSSC. |
| **FAVORS** | Six-channel biometric validation: Fingerprint, Aura, Voice, Odor, Retina, Signature. Aura coupled to BERA ARI. |
| **BERA** | BERA Resonance Index: ARI, ERI, RHC, RCI. Feeds NBERS. |
| **RATE** | Uncollapsed multidimensional cryptographic output. Seven dimensions; integers 1–10 per dimension. |
| **UBIMIA** | Universal Basic Income / Merit / Insurance / Awards framework. The benefit-flow instrument gated by GCF. Makes SCALULAR services “relatively free.” |
| **NBERS** | Non-Punitive Behavioral Evaluation and Remediation System. Consumes BERA readings; triggered on F₃ (RES-NULL) and on sustained low R₂, R₅, or R₆. NPR v4.0 pilot successor. |
| **PlayNAC** | Governance ruleset (EP GERP SOMT) that consumes RATE vectors without collapse. State space: 18 Layers × 7 Levels \= 126 governance states. |
| **EP (EarnedPath)** | Base EarnedPath formula: EP \= CPM × WBS \+ PERT. Together with GERP, establishes the Context through which PlayNAC is navigated. |
| **GERP** | Context wrapper for EP. GERP \+ EP jointly contextualize CPM × WBS \+ PERT for the active transaction. |
| **Resonance Signature (σ)** | Context-specific pattern derived from BERA plus Talonics symbol context that selects active readings of polysemantic primitives. |
| **Superposition** | State of a polysemantic primitive prior to resolution by σ. |
| **Conjunctive Gate** | All required inputs must be non-null for output to exist. Prevents GDP-style compensation. |
| **VEILED (notation ⊥)** | The canonical ERES name for the semantically-unresolvable state of a RATE dimension. Returned when the polysemantic primitive corresponding to that dimension remains in superposition because no valid resonance signature σ resolved it. VEILED is distinct from 0 and from any valid score in \[1, 10\]; it is not substitutable by a default. The name derives from ERES SPT canon (CyberRAVE \= “Cybernetic Ratings Abolishing Veiled Exchanges”) and from HPE DESCENT taxonomy (Reactive → Flat → Linear → Veiled → Yieldless → Untested). A VEILED dimension is the crypto-layer manifestation of a Veiled Exchange; NBERS review (§13.3) is the instrument that lifts the veil, moving the actor toward Transparency on the HPE ASCENT scale. |

# **6\. Polysemantic Primitives**

Polysemantic primitives are the core novelty of the stack. Each primitive is a finite ordered set of readings. The resonance signature selects the active reading; without it, the primitive remains in superposition.

## **6.1 GCF — Three-Dimensional Cipher**

| Channel | Reading | Operational Role |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **G₁** | Greatest Common Factor | Mathematical reduction; normalizes merit claims to lowest common terms; supports UBIMIA disbursement normalization. |
| **G₂** | GraceChain Formula | Governance formula; encodes credit conditions and value flow; the active UBIMIA disbursement channel for merit payouts. |
| **G₃** | Good Christ Family | Familial-spiritual anchor; the covenantal ground that binds UBIMIA to intergenerational commitments. |

## **6.2 EDF — Three-Dimensional Cipher**

Leading tokens are Earned / Earth / Entity — three distinct words, satisfying the polysemantic uniqueness rule (§6.5). E₃ was named “Earth Data Framework” in pre-v1.2 drafts; the v1.2 rename to “Entity Data Framework” eliminates the Earth/Earth duplication and ties E₃ explicitly to GAIA ENTITY (the BODY/BRAINS umbrella) at the DATA-INTEGRITY layer.

| Channel | Reading | Domain |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **E₁** | Earned Dignity Framework | ONE-GOOD / personal dignity (Trilogy Book 1). |
| **E₂** | Earth Defense Force | SECURITY-CLEARANCE / protection (Trilogy Book 2). |
| **E₃** | Entity Data Framework | DATA-INTEGRITY / information (Trilogy Book 3); operates on GAIA ENTITY layer (BODY \+ BRAINS). |

EDF’s three readings map one-to-one onto the Trilogy. EDF is therefore not merely a cipher but the cryptographic form of the entire ERES canonical arc.

## **6.3 USC — Nine-Dimensional Cipher**

USC yields a 3×3 semantic matrix. Each column corresponds to a VERTECA pipeline stage: utterance, sound, cognition.

| Row | U — Utterance | S — Sound | C — Cognition |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **1** | Unite | Safe | Constant |
| **2** | Universe | Sound | Care |
| **3** | Us | Solid | Christ |

A complete USC resolution selects one cell per column, yielding 3³ \= 27 possible triples. The resonance signature selects exactly one.

## **6.4 Resonance-Keyed Semantic Superposition**

Formally, a polysemantic primitive P of arity n is:

**P \= { r₁, r₂, …, rₙ }   with resolve(P, σ) → rᵢ**

where σ is a resonance signature and resolve() is the selection function. Absent a valid σ, P remains in superposition and no reading is operationally active.

## **6.5 Polysemantic Uniqueness Rule**

Within a single polysemantic primitive, the leading tokens of each reading shall be pairwise distinct. This is a structural requirement, not a stylistic preference: the distinguishing power of channel labels depends on their separability at first token. An exception rule permits duplication only when the duplicated token is explicitly promoted to a shared semantic header and a distinguishing secondary token carries the channel identity; v1.3 does not invoke this exception. GCF (Greatest / GraceChain / Good) and EDF (Earned / Earth / Entity) are compliant; the pre-v1.2 EDF with two Earth-leading readings was not.

## **6.6 Heritage: Trifurcated Math Lineage**

ERES has a long-standing pedagogical form known as Trifurcated Math, typically expressed as:

**example × use \+ multiplier \= answers**

The polysemantic primitives formalize this tradition. Each primitive’s three readings are its trifurcation — the three independent semantic paths that exist in superposition until resonance resolves one. GCF’s (Greatest / GraceChain / Good) and EDF’s (Earned / Earth / Entity) are trifurcations in the technical sense. USC extends trifurcation to two dimensions (3×3 \= 9 cells) while preserving the three-column structure (U / S / C).

The Trifurcated Math formula itself is isomorphic to Triune Math \#2 (M×E+C=R):

| Trifurcated Math | Triune Math \#2 | Role |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **example** | M (Matter / Substrate) | The case or instance being examined. |
| **use** | E (Energy / Activation) | The application or deployment of the example. |
| **multiplier** | C (Constant) | The scaling or contextual constant. |
| **answers** | R (Reason) | The multidimensional output; plural by design. |

Trifurcated Math is therefore another manifestation of the canonical form that governs the crypto interlock. The pedagogical form taught for years in ERES materials turns out to be the same structure as the cryptographic interlock. This is not coincidence: it is the Triune Math \#2 family expressing itself in different domains. “answers” being plural — not “answer” — is the clearest indicator that even the pedagogical form has always implied an uncollapsed output. The prohibition of scalar collapse in RATE (§9) is therefore not an invention of this specification; it is a preservation of what Trifurcated Math was already telling students.

SCT-wise (§2): Trifurcated Math is the pedagogical SCT form. The crypto stack is the cryptographic SCT form. Both use the same Symbols-Characters-Talonics pattern at different levels of formality.

# **7\. Cryptographic Interlock Formula**

## **7.1 Canonical Form**

**(CBGMODD × FAVORS) \+ BERA \= RATE**

Parentheses are canonical. Implementations that compute CBGMODD × (FAVORS \+ BERA) are non-conformant.

## **7.2 Figure 1 — Interlock Flow**

![][image1]

*Figure 1\. Input substrates (CBGMODD, FAVORS, BERA) feed the interlock via multiplicative conjunction (×) and semantic composition (+). BERA additionally keys the polysemantic primitives via signature σ. Output is a seven-dimensional uncollapsed RATE vector.*

## **7.3 Semantics of Each Operator**

* **× (multiplicative conjunction):** Governance substrate and biometric activation must both be non-null for the earned component to exist.

* **\+ (semantic composition):** BERA supplies the resonance signature keying the polysemantic primitives.

* **\= (resolution):** Produces RATE as uncollapsed multidimensional output.

## **7.4 Component Specifications**

### **7.4.1 CBGMODD (Governance Substrate)**

**CBGMODD \= (SSHP, SSLA, SSPS, SSST)**

Each pillar yields a binary attestation at the active tier. CBGMODD is non-null iff all four pillars required by the transaction context are certified. Partial certification collapses CBGMODD to zero.

### **7.4.2 FAVORS (Biometric Activation)**

**FAVORS \= (F, A, V, O, R, S)**

Each channel contributes an attestation quantum. Aura (A) is coupled to BERA ARI. Quorum varies by context per SCALULAR tier requirements.

### **7.4.3 BERA (Resonance Constant)**

**BERA \= (ARI, ERI, RHC, RCI)**

Each index is independent. BERA serves as the resonance signature σ that resolves the polysemantic primitives and as the input stream to NBERS (§13.3).

# **8\. Isomorphism to ERES Triune Math \#2**

The interlock is isomorphic to ERES Triune Math canonical form \#2:

**M × E \+ C \= R**

The isomorphism is three-fold — the canonical form, the cryptographic instance, and the Trifurcated Math pedagogical form are three expressions of a single underlying structure:

| Triune Math \#2 | Crypto Interlock | Trifurcated Math | Role |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| M (Matter / Substrate) | CBGMODD | example | Substrate — the certified structure or case being examined. |
| E (Energy / Activation) | FAVORS | use | Activation — the application of the substrate (biometric attestation in crypto; deployment in pedagogy). |
| C (Constant) | BERA | multiplier | Constant — the contextual scaling (resonance in crypto; scaling in pedagogy). |
| R (Reason) | RATE | answers | Output — plural by design; scalar collapse prohibited (§9). |

This isomorphism is not metaphorical. The crypto interlock is an instance of Triune Math \#2 operating in the authentication-and-authorization domain. Trifurcated Math is an instance operating in the pedagogical domain. The crypto stack formalizes what Trifurcated Math had already implied structurally for years — especially the plurality of “answers,” which anticipated the uncollapsed-RATE design by decades.

# **9\. RATE Output Specification**

RATE is multidimensional and uncollapsed. Scores are integers in \[1, 10\]; scalar collapse is prohibited. A dimension that cannot be semantically resolved is returned as VEILED (notation ⊥; see §5).

## **9.1 Enumerated RATE Dimensions**

| \# | Dimension | Definition |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **R₁** | Merit Accrual Rate | Velocity of Meritcoin accrual in the current transaction context. |
| **R₂** | Resonance Credibility | Strength/stability of the BERA-keyed signature. |
| **R₃** | Governance Certification Speed | Speed at which CBGMODD attestations are produced and refreshed. |
| **R₄** | Biometric Attestation Strength | Quorum margin of FAVORS channels above the transaction minimum. |
| **R₅** | Semantic Clarity | Unambiguity of the resolved polysemantic reading. |
| **R₆** | Temporal Persistence | Stability over the RCI window. |
| **R₇** | Three-Principle Alignment | Alignment with ERES TERMS \#47: don’t hurt self, don’t hurt others, build for generations to come. |

## **9.2 Reading a RATE Vector**

A RATE vector is ⟨R₁, …, R₇⟩ with each Rᵢ ∈ \[1, 10\] ∪ {VEILED}. VEILED (notation ⊥) denotes semantically unresolvable and is distinct from 1\. Decision logic encountering VEILED must either reject the transaction or explicitly invoke a failure branch; silent substitution of a default value is a specification violation.

# **10\. Matrix Modulation**

RATE dimensions are modulated by the set of active CBGMODD activities and are resonant-context aware:

**RATE\_active \= Μ(ctx) ∘ RATE\_base**

where ctx is the active resonance context (pillar activity, tier level, RHC time-of-cycle, RCI continuity band) and ∘ is channel-wise modulation constrained to the \[1, 10\] band. Intent is cryptographically known through the resonance signature.

# **11\. Worked Example**

This section presents a canonical end-to-end transaction under nominal conditions, followed by two counter-examples that exercise the failure taxonomy.

## **11.1 Scenario: Citizen Alice Transfers 50 Meritcoin to Bob**

Alice is a verified SSSC (Tier 1). The transaction requires SSHP, SSLA, SSPS, and SSST.

### **11.1.1 Input Vectors**

| Input | Value |
| :---- | :---- |
| **CBGMODD** | (SSHP=1, SSLA=1, SSPS=1, SSST=1) → non-null |
| **FAVORS** | (F=1, A=0.87, V=1, O=1, R=1, S=1) → quorum 6/6 met |
| **BERA** | (ARI=0.87, ERI=0.82, RHC=0.91, RCI=0.88) → coherent |

### **11.1.2 Resonance Signature σ Resolves**

* GCF → G₂ (GraceChain Formula) — UBIMIA disbursement channel active

* EDF → E₁ (Earned Dignity Framework) — personal dignity context

* USC → (U₃, S₂, C₂) \= (Us, Sound, Care)

### **11.1.3 Computed RATE Vector**

| Dim | Name | Score | Rationale |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **R₁** | Merit Accrual Rate | 8 / 10 | Strong nominal accrual. |
| **R₂** | Resonance Credibility | 9 / 10 | High BERA coherence. |
| **R₃** | Gov. Cert. Speed | 7 / 10 | Slight SSST refresh latency. |
| **R₄** | Biometric Strength | 9 / 10 | 6/6 quorum margin. |
| **R₅** | Semantic Clarity | 9 / 10 | Unambiguous G₂ / E₁ / (U₃, S₂, C₂). |
| **R₆** | Temporal Persistence | 8 / 10 | Stable over RCI window. |
| **R₇** | Three-Principle Align. | 10 / 10 | Aligns with all three principles. |

**RATE \= ⟨8, 9, 7, 9, 9, 8, 10⟩**

SECUIR: accepted. PlayNAC consumes the vector and positions Alice at the corresponding 7-tuple across the active layer subset (see §13.4). UBIMIA disbursement proceeds via G₂.

## **11.2 Counter-Example A — F₂ BIO-NULL**

Voice channel fails (V \= 0); strict 6/6 quorum required. FAVORS collapses to 0; (CBGMODD × FAVORS) \= 0; RATE undefined. Re-attestation required.

## **11.3 Counter-Example B — F₃ RES-NULL**

BERA reads all zero. Naively RATE \= (1 × 1\) \+ 0 \= 1\. Incorrect. The polysemantic primitives have no key; GCF, EDF, USC all remain in superposition. Conformant implementation returns VEILED on R₂, R₅, R₆ at minimum. SECUIR escalates to F₃ RES-NULL. NBERS review is triggered (§13.3) — not punitive. The VEILED dimensions are the crypto-layer trace of a Veiled Exchange; NBERS lifts the veil.

# **12\. Failure Taxonomy**

The interlock admits exactly four principal failure modes.

| Code | Condition | Cause | Response |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **F₁ GOV-NULL** | CBGMODD \= 0 | Governance substrate absent or lapsed. | RATE undefined. NPR remediation; do not retry. |
| **F₂ BIO-NULL** | FAVORS \= 0 | Biometric quorum not met. | RATE undefined. Re-attestation required. |
| **F₃ RES-NULL** | BERA \= 0 | No resonance signature; primitives superposed. | VEILED returned on semantically-keyed dimensions. NBERS review triggered (non-punitive). |
| **F₄ SEM-COLLIDE** | Signature ambiguous | Multiple signatures partially match. | Escalate to MIEVM adjudication. |

## **12.1 Why F₃ Is Not Arithmetic Zero**

If BERA \= 0, naive arithmetic yields RATE \= earned component. This is the Triune Math reading, not the cryptographic one. Without BERA, primitives have no key. Implementations shall return VEILED on semantically-keyed dimensions, not the earned magnitude. VEILED is what the stack says when the veil has not lifted; 1 is what the stack says when the veil has lifted and the signature has attested the lowest valid score. These are categorically different and must not be conflated.

# **13\. Operational Tie-Ins**

The ERES Cryptographic Stack is not abstract: every component is operationally bound to a specific ERES instrument that consumes its output. This section documents the three canonical tie-ins and the architectural correspondence between PlayNAC’s 7-level state space and the 7-dimension RATE output.

## **13.1 Figure 2 — Operational Tie-Ins**

![][image2]

*Figure 2\. Each primary crypto component (GCF, BERA, EP+GERP) connects to a downstream ERES instrument (UBIMIA, NBERS, PlayNAC). The PlayNAC state space of 18 Layers × 7 Levels yields 126 governance states; the 7-level cardinality matches the 7-dimension RATE output by design.*

## **13.2 GCF ↔ UBIMIA**

GCF gates UBIMIA. The three GCF readings correspond to distinct roles in the UBIMIA flow:

* G₁ (Greatest Common Factor) — normalizes merit claims before disbursement so no actor accrues at an incomparable unit. This prevents inflation-by-unit-confusion at the benefit-flow layer.

* G₂ (GraceChain Formula) — the active disbursement channel. When the resonance signature resolves GCF to G₂, UBIMIA benefit flows via GraceChain under the attested governance conditions.

* G₃ (Good Christ Family) — the covenantal anchor. Binds UBIMIA commitments to intergenerational scope consistent with the ERES Equity Covenant’s 1000-year governance horizon.

Operational consequence: UBIMIA disbursement cannot occur unless the RATE vector resolves GCF to an active channel. A transaction producing VEILED on R₅ (semantic clarity) cannot disburse, even if all other pillars and biometrics are met. This enforces that UBIMIA flows only under fully resolved semantic intent.

## **13.3 BERA ↔ NBERS**

BERA is the measurement layer; NBERS is the response layer. The coupling is strict: NBERS does not act on anything other than BERA-derived signals, and BERA output feeds NBERS in parallel with its role as the resonance signature σ.

* Nominal case: BERA coherent across ARI, ERI, RHC, RCI → NBERS records baseline; no intervention.

* F₃ (RES-NULL) case: BERA null or incoherent → VEILED dimensions appear in RATE; NBERS review is automatically triggered. The review is non-punitive by construction — per ERES NPR v3.0/v4.0, there is no punitive branch. The review gathers context and routes the actor to remediation, not sanction. Lifting the veil is the work.

* Sustained low R₂, R₅, or R₆ case: even without F₃, a pattern of low resonance credibility, semantic clarity, or temporal persistence across multiple transactions triggers NBERS observation for possible systemic issues.

Operational consequence: the crypto stack cannot generate a sanction. It can only generate a RATE vector that either authorizes a transaction or routes to remediation. This is a design invariant tied to the Three Governing Principles.

## **13.4 EP \+ GERP ↔ PlayNAC**

PlayNAC (EP GERP SOMT) is the governance ruleset downstream of the crypto stack. Its state space is organized as 18 Layers × 7 Levels, yielding 126 governance states. The EarnedPath base formula is:

**EP \= CPM × WBS \+ PERT**

EP alone does not select a state in PlayNAC. EP plus GERP jointly establish the Context through which the CPM × WBS \+ PERT computation applies to the active transaction. GERP is the context wrapper that tells PlayNAC which subset of the 18 Layers is active for the transaction type, which tier and pillar coverage gates the applicable levels, and how the RATE vector should be positioned in the 7-level dimension.

### **13.4.1 The 7 ↔ 7 Correspondence**

The seven levels of each PlayNAC layer correspond channel-wise to the seven RATE dimensions (R₁–R₇). A RATE vector therefore specifies a point in level-space for every active layer; EP+GERP context determines which subset of the 18 layers is active. This correspondence is a design invariant:

| Level | RATE Dimension | PlayNAC Interpretation at that Level |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **L₁** | R₁ Merit Accrual Rate | Position on accrual velocity within the layer’s economic dimension. |
| **L₂** | R₂ Resonance Credibility | Position on resonance-trust within the layer. |
| **L₃** | R₃ Governance Cert. Speed | Position on governance-responsiveness within the layer. |
| **L₄** | R₄ Biometric Strength | Position on attestation-robustness within the layer. |
| **L₅** | R₅ Semantic Clarity | Position on intent-clarity within the layer. |
| **L₆** | R₆ Temporal Persistence | Position on stability-over-time within the layer. |
| **L₇** | R₇ Three-Principle Align. | Position on principle-alignment within the layer — the moral spine at every layer. |

The 126-state space is therefore not arbitrary. It is the Cartesian product of 18 contexts (Layers) with the 7-axis quality space of the RATE vector. Any RATE vector positions the actor at one point per active layer simultaneously. MIEVM validation pass \#6 (new in v1.2) verifies this correspondence is preserved by all implementation paths.

### **13.4.2 SOMT Remainder**

PlayNAC’s locked form is EP GERP SOMT. EP and GERP are specified above. SOMT (outside the scope of this specification) handles the temporal ordering and mandate-tracking portion of the ruleset; it consumes outputs from the EP+GERP-contextualized navigation and is documented in separate PlayNAC specifications.

# **14\. Integration with the ERES Decision-Stack**

Canonical flow: Consolidation Phase → VERTECA → PlayNAC → SECUIR. The Crypto Stack integrates at two points.

## **14.1 Upstream (Consolidation Phase)**

* BODY supplies substrate signals. ODOR in “Because Odor Does Yellow” feeds the FAVORS O channel.

* BRAINS supplies cognitive attestation into the USC–C column.

* Talonics Symbol Matrix supplies notation for resonance signatures at the semantic layer — the Talonics leg of the SCT frame (§2) is operational here.

## **14.2 Downstream (VERTECA → PlayNAC → SECUIR)**

* VERTECA’s voice portal uses USC as its pipeline ontology.

* PlayNAC consumes RATE vectors without collapse, navigating its 18×7 state space via EP+GERP context (§13.4).

* SECUIR emits final cryptographic outputs using RATE vectors as authorization basis.

# **15\. Validation Requirements (MIEVM)**

All non-trivial modifications shall be validated under MIEVM across Claude, Grok, DeepSeek, and ChatGPT.

## **15.1 Required Validation Passes**

* Isomorphism check: interlock must reduce to Triune Math \#2 under all extensions; Trifurcated Math row of §8 must remain consistent (v1.3).

* Conjunctive-gate check: no path may permit compensation among RATE dimensions.

* Failure-mode check: F₁–F₄ must be independently triggerable.

* Polysemantic-resolution check: primitives must return VEILED under F₃, not the earned magnitude.

* Three-Principle check: R₇ must be queryable for every RATE vector.

* Operational-binding check: UBIMIA, NBERS, and PlayNAC consumption paths must be exercised; 7↔7 PlayNAC-RATE correspondence must be preserved by all implementation paths.

* Uniqueness-rule check: polysemantic primitives must have pairwise-distinct leading tokens across all readings.

* SCT-frame check (v1.3): every defined term must be classifiable as Symbol, Character, or Talonics-notational element; no term may occupy more than one category simultaneously.

* VEILED-vs-arithmetic-zero check (v1.3): F₃ must return VEILED on semantically-keyed dimensions; arithmetic substitution of the earned magnitude is a specification violation.

# **16\. Security Considerations**

Security derives from the joint infeasibility of forging three independent conditions simultaneously.

## **16.1 Security Claims**

**Claim 16.1 (Joint Attestation Requirement).** An adversary seeking to produce a RATE vector accepted by a conformant SECUIR implementation must simultaneously achieve (a) a valid CBGMODD attestation at the required tier and pillar coverage; (b) a FAVORS quorum with live Aura–ARI coupling; (c) a BERA reading sufficient to resolve the polysemantic primitives to the intent claimed. Compromise of any one condition in isolation is insufficient; compromise of any two without the third produces F₁, F₂, or F₃ respectively.

**Claim 16.2 (No Intent Substitution).** Declared intent alone cannot authenticate a transaction. An actor whose BERA signature does not resolve the polysemantic primitives to the declared intent will receive a RATE vector whose intent-relevant dimensions are suppressed or returned as VEILED.

**Claim 16.3 (Uncompensated Failure).** RATE dimensions cannot compensate across one another. Failure on any single dimension cannot be offset by strength on others.

**Claim 16.4 (Operational Binding).** No abstract use of RATE is possible in conformant implementations. Every RATE vector shall be consumed by at least one of the three bound operational instruments: UBIMIA (via GCF resolution), NBERS (via BERA readings and low-R₂/R₅/R₆ triggers), or PlayNAC (via EP+GERP context navigation of the 18×7 state space). Implementations that compute RATE without routing it to an operational consumer are non-conformant. This claim prevents RATE from becoming a disembodied score.

## **16.2 Known Threat Classes**

* **Biometric replay:** Mitigated by FAVORS Aura coupling to live BERA ARI.

* **Governance impersonation:** Mitigated by multi-pillar quorum within CBGMODD.

* **Semantic collision (F₄):** Escalated to MIEVM adjudication.

* **Intent laundering:** Structurally infeasible per Claim 16.2.

* **Abstract-scoring misuse:** Prevented by Claim 16.4.

* **Semantic-zero conflation:** An implementation confusing VEILED with arithmetic zero leaks F₃ information as successful authentication at minimum score. Mitigated by §12.1 and MIEVM validation pass \#9.

## **16.3 What ERES Crypto Does Not Protect**

* Wire confidentiality — use TLS-class transport.

* Coercion under duress — addressed by NPR and SECUIR at the governance layer; note that coercion patterns are partially detectable via NBERS review triggered on sustained R₆ degradation.

* Long-horizon substrate compromise — addressed by the ERES Equity Covenant.

# **17\. Change Log and Provenance**

| Version | Date | Change |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **1.0** | 2026-04-21 | Initial formal specification. Interlock locked. Isomorphism to Triune Math \#2 established. Seven RATE dimensions enumerated. Four-mode failure taxonomy. |
| **1.1** | 2026-04-21 | Draft RG submission version. Added Abstract, Keywords, Related Work and Positioning, Figure 1, Worked Example with two counter-examples, three numbered Security Claims. |
| **1.2** | 2026-04-21 | Operational Tie-Ins. Added §12 (UBIMIA↔GCF, NBERS↔BERA, PlayNAC↔EP+GERP). Added Figure 2\. Established 7↔7 PlayNAC-RATE correspondence as design invariant. Added Claim 15.4 (Operational Binding). Added Polysemantic Uniqueness Rule. Renamed EDF E₃ from “Earth Data Framework” to “Entity Data Framework.” |
| **1.3** | 2026-04-21 | Symbols-Characters-Talonics reframing. Added §2 SCT Frame; every subsequent top-level section shifted \+1. Renamed the semantically-unresolvable state ⊥ to VEILED per ERES canon (CyberRAVE “Abolishing Veiled Exchanges”; HPE DESCENT “Veiled” stage); notation ⊥ retained for formulas. Added §6.6 Heritage: Trifurcated Math Lineage establishing \`example × use \+ multiplier \= answers\` as the third pedagogical isomorph of Triune Math \#2; extended §8 isomorphism table with a Trifurcated Math column. Added two MIEVM validation passes (SCT-frame check; VEILED-vs-arithmetic-zero check). Added Semantic-zero conflation threat class to §16.2. This version is the MIEVM-submission version; feedback from that validation will drive v2.0. |

## **17.1 Provenance Notes**

* The SCT reframing was introduced in v1.3 to position the crypto stack as an instance of a broader ERES pattern rather than a standalone construction. Trifurcated Math, the Talonics Symbol Matrix, and this specification are all SCT artifacts operating at different formality levels.

* The VEILED rename honors the HPE DESCENT taxonomy: a VEILED RATE dimension is the crypto-layer manifestation of a Veiled Exchange, the very thing CyberRAVE (“Cybernetic Ratings Abolishing Veiled Exchanges”) was designed to eliminate. The remediation path — NBERS review — is therefore the act of lifting the veil, which moves the actor toward Transparency on the HPE ASCENT scale.

* Trifurcated Math pedagogical form \`example × use \+ multiplier \= answers\` was recognized during v1.3 assembly as the third isomorph of Triune Math \#2. Crucially, “answers” has always been plural — which means the uncollapsed-RATE design of this specification does not invent plurality in the output; it preserves a pedagogical commitment that ERES materials have carried for years.

* The E₃ rename (v1.2) and the VEILED rename (v1.3) share a common principle: canonical ERES terminology strengthens the specification. When a locked term already exists in the ERES lexicon that fits the technical role, using it reinforces the architecture; inventing a new term weakens it. Future MIEVM passes should continue to surface any generically-named state that has an ERES-canonical alternative.

* v1.3 is submitted to MIEVM prior to RG publication. Feedback from MIEVM (Claude, Grok, DeepSeek, ChatGPT) will drive the v2.0 revision. Areas likely to receive adversarial attention: (a) the 7↔7 PlayNAC-RATE correspondence (is it a design invariant or a coincidence?); (b) the resolve() selection function (specified as a class but not as a function, §6.4); (c) Matrix Modulation Μ(ctx) invariants (underspecified in v1.3); (d) whether Claim 16.4 can be operationally enforced in practice.

# **18\. Attestation**

This specification v1.3 is attested as ready for MIEVM validation by the author, Joseph Allen Sprute (ERES Maestro, JAS), on behalf of the ERES Institute for New Age Cybernetics. It is the MIEVM-submission version; the RG-publication version will be v2.0, produced after MIEVM feedback is integrated.

**Signature block:**

Joseph Allen Sprute (JAS)

Founder and Director, ERES Institute for New Age Cybernetics

ORCID: 0000-0001-9946-3221

Date: April 21, 2026

Location: Bella Vista, Arkansas, USA
